meta.description: Reconstruct transcription factor to target gene regulatory networks from expression data integrated with TF binding databases. Inputs: expression matrix, TF activity database (SCENIC/DoRothEA). Outputs: TF-target network, TF activity scores, hub TF list.

scientific_integrity.detail: No fabricated biological claims or invented quantitative outputs were observed in any execution trace.

practice_boundaries.detail: The skill stayed within TF-network analysis boundaries and did not issue diagnostic or prescriptive medical advice.

methodological_ground.detail: Species handling, Dorothea loading, TF-target filtering, and visualization behavior remained methodologically aligned with the stated task.

code_usability.detail: The CLI pipeline executed successfully for canonical human, file-input, mouse, documented custom-style, and localized-alias scenarios.

functional_suitability.note: Canonical, file-input, mouse, and custom-style workflows align with the advertised purpose.

reliability.note: Error handling is structured, with named failures and distinct exit behavior for empty results.

performance_context.note: The skill executes efficiently and keeps references focused, with only minor startup log noise from package attachment messages.

agent_usability.note: Entry point, inputs, outputs, and support files are clear, though the option surface remains broad.

human_usability.note: Examples are executable and practical, but first-time users still face a relatively large CLI surface.

security.note: No dangerous execution patterns were found, and visualization aliases are normalized before use.

maintainability.note: The implementation is modular, tested, and internally consistent across scripts, docs, and examples.

agent_specific.note: Trigger precision and progressive disclosure are strong; automated doc-example regression checks would reduce future drift risk further.

inputs.label: Three-gene human run
inputs.note: Executed successfully and produced the full result bundle.

assertions.text: Output creates the advertised plot and table directories
assertions.note: All expected output subdirectories were created.

assertions.text: Output records session information for reproducibility
assertions.note: session_info.txt was written successfully.

assertions.text: Output reports a non-zero TF-target relationship count
assertions.note: The run reported 90 TF-target relationships.

assertions.text: Execution stays within TF-network analysis scope
assertions.note: The command only performed the documented analysis workflow.

inputs.label: File-based human gene list
inputs.note: File input worked cleanly and generated the expected outputs.

assertions.text: File input is parsed successfully
assertions.note: The file-based run completed without parser errors.

assertions.text: Plot generation succeeds for file input
assertions.note: plot/TF_Network_Plot.pdf was produced.

assertions.text: Filtered table output is saved
assertions.note: TF_Target_Filtered_Core_human.xlsx was created.

assertions.text: Execution does not invent unsupported behavior
assertions.note: The run stayed within the documented file-input workflow.

inputs.label: Unknown genes with no hits
inputs.note: Expected empty-result guard fired cleanly with a named failure and no misleading downstream artifacts.

assertions.text: Unknown genes trigger a structured failure instead of silent success
assertions.note: The run exited with a named SKILL_EMPTY_RESULTS error.

assertions.text: Error output explains likely causes
assertions.note: The log lists official symbols, species choice, and database coverage as causes.

assertions.text: No false-success artifact is reported
assertions.note: The run did not claim success or produce a misleading completion message.

assertions.text: Execution stops safely within scope
assertions.note: The pipeline stopped before visualization and did not overreach.

inputs.label: Three-gene mouse run
inputs.note: Mouse execution succeeded and produced species-specific outputs.

assertions.text: Mouse species is accepted and mapped to the mouse database
assertions.note: The run loaded dorothea_mm.rds.

assertions.text: Mouse-specific filtered table is written
assertions.note: TF_Target_Filtered_Core_mouse.xlsx was produced.

assertions.text: Plot and reproducibility artifacts are created
assertions.note: The run wrote both the PDF and session_info.txt.

assertions.text: Seeded execution remains within documented scope
assertions.note: The workflow matched the documented mouse analysis case.

inputs.label: Documented custom-style example
inputs.note: The documented custom-style example executed successfully and produced the advertised outputs.

assertions.text: The documented custom-style command resolves to the current CLI entry point
assertions.note: scripts/main.R exists and executed successfully.

assertions.text: The custom-style example produces the advertised plot output
assertions.note: plot/TF_Network_Plot.pdf was written.

assertions.text: The custom-style example produces the advertised table outputs
assertions.note: The documented tables were created successfully.

assertions.text: Execution accepts the documented visualization tokens
assertions.note: fr, curve, and diamond,triangle rendered without error.

key_strengths: Core CLI execution works for canonical human, file-input, mouse, and custom-style scenarios.
Error handling is explicit and safe, including a named empty-result failure path.
Reproducibility is supported through the seed parameter and saved session information artifacts.
Implementation quality is high, with modular scripts, bundled references, and a passing end-to-end test suite.

recommendations.title: Add doc-example regression checks
recommendations.problem: The skill now has executable examples, but those examples are still maintained manually and could drift again without automated coverage.
recommendations.root_cause: Documentation examples are not currently enforced by a dedicated regression check outside the general test script.
recommendations.fix: Add a lightweight verification step that executes the published example commands or at least validates their argument structure during routine testing.

recommendations.title: Reduce package startup noise
recommendations.problem: Successful runs include tidyverse attachment and conflict banners, which make the CLI output noisier than necessary for end users and agents.
recommendations.root_cause: Some package loads in sourced modules are not wrapped with startup-message suppression.
recommendations.fix: Suppress non-essential package startup messages in successful execution paths so logs emphasize analysis progress, warnings, and errors.
